full_scripture: Now there arose up a new king over Egypt, which knew not Joseph. And he said unto his people, Behold, the people of the children of Israel are more and mightier than we: Come on, let us deal wisely with them; lest they multiply.
speaker: Pharaoh
conditions: Because Israel had multiplied greatly in Egypt
command_summary: Deal wisely with the Israelites to restrain their growth
interpretation_notes: Pharaoh gives a direct political instruction motivated by fear of Israel's increase.

full_scripture: Therefore they did set over them taskmasters to afflict them with their burdens.
speaker: Narrator
command_summary: Israel is oppressed with forced labor
interpretation_notes: Narrative consequence of Pharaoh's policy rather than a spoken imperative.

full_scripture: And the king of Egypt spake to the Hebrew midwives... When ye do the office of a midwife to the Hebrew women... if it be a son, then ye shall kill him.
speaker: Pharaoh
conditions: When Hebrew women give birth to sons
command_summary: Kill Hebrew male children at birth
interpretation_notes: Direct state-sponsored infanticide order from Pharaoh.

full_scripture: But the midwives feared God, and did not as the king of Egypt commanded them, but saved the men children alive.
speaker: Narrator
command_summary: Fear God rather than obey wicked commands
interpretation_notes: An inferred moral principle demonstrated through narrative action.

full_scripture: And Pharaoh charged all his people, saying, Every son that is born ye shall cast into the river.
speaker: Pharaoh
conditions: Upon the birth of Hebrew sons
command_summary: Cast Hebrew sons into the river
interpretation_notes: Escalation of Pharaoh's anti-Israelite decree to the entire population.

full_scripture: And there went a man of the house of Levi, and took to wife a daughter of Levi. And the woman conceived, and bare a son... and his sister stood afar off, to wit what would be done to him.
speaker: Narrator
command_summary: Moses is preserved by faith through his mother
interpretation_notes: Narrative preservation account establishing Moses as divinely protected deliverer.

full_scripture: And the daughter of Pharaoh came down to wash herself at the river... and he became her son. And she called his name Moses.
speaker: Narrator
command_summary: Pharaoh's daughter adopts Moses
interpretation_notes: God's providence works through an Egyptian royal figure to preserve Moses.

full_scripture: And it came to pass in those days, when Moses was grown... and he slew the Egyptian, and hid him in the sand.
speaker: Narrator
command_summary: Moses kills an Egyptian oppressor
interpretation_notes: Moses attempts deliverance through human force before divine commissioning.

full_scripture: And when he went out the second day, behold, two men of the Hebrews strove together: and he said to him that did the wrong, Wherefore smitest thou thy fellow?
speaker: Moses
command_summary: Moses rebukes violence among Hebrews
interpretation_notes: Moses acts as mediator and moral rebuker among his own people.

full_scripture: Now when Pharaoh heard this thing, he sought to slay Moses. But Moses fled from the face of Pharaoh, and dwelt in the land of Midian.
speaker: Narrator
conditions: Pharaoh learns of the Egyptian's death
command_summary: Moses flees from Pharaoh into Midian
interpretation_notes: Moses enters exile prior to divine commissioning.

full_scripture: Now the priest of Midian had seven daughters... and Moses stood up and helped them, and watered their flock.
speaker: Narrator
command_summary: Moses defends the daughters of Midian
interpretation_notes: Moses demonstrates protective leadership prior to formal calling.

full_scripture: And when they came to Reuel their father... and she bare him a son, and he called his name Gershom.
speaker: Reuel
command_summary: Reuel invites Moses into his household
interpretation_notes: Contains an explicit invitation command from Reuel to Moses.

full_scripture: And it came to pass in process of time, that the king of Egypt died... and God heard their groaning, and God remembered his covenant with Abraham, with Isaac, and with Jacob.
speaker: Narrator
command_summary: God remembers His covenant with Israel
interpretation_notes: Major covenant-transition setup leading into the Exodus deliverance narrative.

full_scripture: And he said, Draw not nigh hither: put off thy shoes from off thy feet, for the place whereon thou standest is holy ground.
speaker: God
conditions: while approaching the burning bush on holy ground
command_summary: Do not approach casually and remove sandals on holy ground
interpretation_notes: The command establishes sacred separation and reverence before God's presence.

full_scripture: Moreover he said, I am the God of thy father, the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob. And Moses hid his face; for he was afraid to look upon God.
speaker: Narrator
command_summary: Respond reverently before God's revealed holiness
interpretation_notes: No explicit command is spoken, but Moses models reverent fear before divine holiness.

full_scripture: Come now therefore, and I will send thee unto Pharaoh, that thou mayest bring forth my people the children of Israel out of Egypt.
speaker: God
command_summary: Go to Pharaoh and deliver Israel from Egypt
interpretation_notes: This is Moses' formal divine commissioning as deliverer of Israel.

full_scripture: Go, and gather the elders of Israel together, and say unto them, The LORD God of your fathers... hath visited you, and seen that which is done to you in Egypt.
speaker: God
command_summary: Gather Israel's elders and declare God's concern for them
interpretation_notes: God instructs Moses to establish covenantal legitimacy before Israel.

full_scripture: And thou shalt come, thou and the elders of Israel, unto the king of Egypt, and ye shall say unto him, The LORD God of the Hebrews hath met with us: and now let us go...
speaker: God
conditions: after speaking with the elders of Israel
command_summary: Approach Pharaoh and request permission to worship God
interpretation_notes: The request to Pharaoh centers on covenant worship obligations.

full_scripture: But every woman shall borrow of her neighbour... jewels of silver, and jewels of gold, and raiment...
speaker: God
command_summary: Request valuables from the Egyptians before departing
interpretation_notes: The action functions as divine compensation for Israel's bondage rather than theft.

full_scripture: And Moses answered and said, But, behold, they will not believe me, nor hearken unto my voice: for they will say, The LORD hath not appeared unto thee.
speaker: Moses
conditions: If Israel refuses to believe Moses
command_summary: Moses fears Israel will reject his message
interpretation_notes: Moses expresses doubt regarding his prophetic credibility before Israel.

full_scripture: And the LORD said unto him, What is that in thine hand? And he said, A rod. And he said, Cast it on the ground...
speaker: God
command_summary: God commands Moses to cast down the rod
interpretation_notes: The rod sign functions as divine authentication of Moses.

full_scripture: And the LORD said furthermore unto him, Put now thine hand into thy bosom...
speaker: God
command_summary: God commands Moses to perform the leprous hand sign
interpretation_notes: The miraculous healing sign reinforces God's power over impurity.

full_scripture: And it shall come to pass, if they will not believe also these two signs... thou shalt take of the water of the river...
speaker: God
conditions: If Israel rejects the first two signs
command_summary: God instructs Moses to use the Nile sign if needed
interpretation_notes: The Nile judgment anticipates later Egyptian plagues.

full_scripture: And Moses said unto the LORD, O my Lord, I am not eloquent...
speaker: Moses
command_summary: Moses objects due to lack of eloquence
interpretation_notes: Moses emphasizes personal inadequacy before divine calling.

full_scripture: And the LORD said unto him, Who hath made man's mouth?... now therefore go, and I will be with thy mouth...
speaker: God
command_summary: God commands Moses to go and promises help
interpretation_notes: God directly answers Moses' insecurity with divine assurance.

full_scripture: And he said, O my Lord, send, I pray thee, by the hand of him whom thou wilt send...
speaker: God
command_summary: God appoints Aaron and commands Moses to take the rod
interpretation_notes: Aaron becomes Moses' spokesman while Moses retains prophetic authority.

full_scripture: And afterward Moses and Aaron went in, and told Pharaoh, Thus saith the LORD God of Israel, Let my people go, that they may hold a feast unto me in the wilderness.
speaker: Moses
conditions: that they may hold a feast unto me in the wilderness
command_summary: Let Israel go to worship in the wilderness
interpretation_notes: Direct divine demand delivered through Moses and Aaron to Pharaoh.

full_scripture: And they said, The God of the Hebrews hath met with us: let us go, we pray thee, three days' journey into the desert, and sacrifice unto the LORD our God; lest he fall upon us with pestilence, or with the sword.
speaker: Moses
conditions: lest he fall upon us with pestilence, or with the sword
command_summary: Permit Israel to journey and sacrifice to God
interpretation_notes: The request includes both command and warning elements tied to covenant obedience.

full_scripture: And the king of Egypt said unto them, Wherefore do ye, Moses and Aaron, let the people from their works? get you unto your burdens.
speaker: Pharaoh
command_summary: Return to your labor burdens
interpretation_notes: Pharaoh rejects divine authority and reinforces forced labor.

full_scripture: And Pharaoh commanded the same day the taskmasters of the people, and their officers, saying, Ye shall no more give the people straw to make brick, as heretofore: let them go and gather straw for themselves.
speaker: Pharaoh
command_summary: Stop providing straw for brickmaking
interpretation_notes: This intensifies Israel's bondage after Moses' request.

full_scripture: And the tale of the bricks, which they did make heretofore, ye shall lay upon them; ye shall not diminish ought thereof.
speaker: Pharaoh
command_summary: Do not reduce the brick quota
interpretation_notes: Pharaoh increases oppression while maintaining production demands.

full_scripture: Let there more work be laid upon the men, that they may labour therein; and let them not regard vain words.
speaker: Pharaoh
command_summary: Increase labor to suppress attention to Moses
interpretation_notes: Pharaoh attempts to counteract divine revelation through increased oppression.

full_scripture: But he said, Ye are idle, ye are idle: therefore ye say, Let us go and do sacrifice to the LORD. Go therefore now, and work.
speaker: Pharaoh
command_summary: Return to work instead of worship
interpretation_notes: Pharaoh directly opposes Israel's call to sacrificial worship.

full_scripture: And God spake unto Moses, and said unto him, I am the LORD: And I appeared unto Abraham, unto Isaac, and unto Jacob, by the name of God Almighty, but by my name JEHOVAH was I not known to them. And I have also established my covenant with them, to give them the land of Canaan, the land of their pilgrimage, wherein they were strangers. And I have also heard the groaning of the children of Israel, whom the Egyptians keep in bondage; and I have remembered my covenant. Wherefore say unto the children of Israel, I am the LORD, and I will bring you out from under the burdens of the Egyptians, and I will rid you out of their bondage, and I will redeem you with a stretched out arm, and with great judgments: And I will take you to me for a people, and I will be to you a God: and ye shall know that I am the LORD your God, which bringeth you out from under the burdens of the Egyptians. And I will bring you in unto the land, concerning the which I did swear to give it to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob; and I will give it you for an heritage: I am the LORD.
speaker: God
command_summary: God declares His covenant faithfulness and promise to deliver Israel
interpretation_notes: Central covenant reaffirmation connecting patriarchal promises to the Exodus redemption.

full_scripture: Wherefore say unto the children of Israel, I am the LORD, and I will bring you out from under the burdens of the Egyptians, and I will rid you out of their bondage, and I will redeem you with a stretched out arm, and with great judgments:
speaker: God
command_summary: Tell Israel that God will redeem them from Egypt
interpretation_notes: God commands Moses to proclaim divine redemption to Israel.

full_scripture: And I will take you to me for a people, and I will be to you a God: and ye shall know that I am the LORD your God, which bringeth you out from under the burdens of the Egyptians.
speaker: God
command_summary: God promises covenant relationship with Israel
interpretation_notes: Introduces the covenant formula repeated throughout scripture: 'I will be your God, you shall be my people.'

full_scripture: And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying, Go in, speak unto Pharaoh king of Egypt, that he let the children of Israel go out of his land.
speaker: God
command_summary: Moses is commanded to confront Pharaoh
interpretation_notes: Repeated commissioning formula reinforcing Moses' prophetic authority.

full_scripture: And the LORD spake unto Moses and unto Aaron, and gave them a charge unto the children of Israel, and unto Pharaoh king of Egypt, to bring the children of Israel out of the land of Egypt.
speaker: God
command_summary: Moses and Aaron receive charge to lead Israel out of Egypt
interpretation_notes: Formal commissioning of both Moses and Aaron before the escalation with Pharaoh.

full_scripture: And the LORD said unto Moses, See, I have made thee a god to Pharaoh: and Aaron thy brother shall be thy prophet. Thou shalt speak all that I command thee: and Aaron thy brother shall speak unto Pharaoh, that he send the children of Israel out of his land.
speaker: God
command_summary: Moses must speak all God commands
interpretation_notes: Direct commissioning command from God to Moses concerning Pharaoh.

full_scripture: And I will harden Pharaoh's heart, and multiply my signs and my wonders in the land of Egypt. But Pharaoh shall not hearken unto you, that I may lay my hand upon Egypt... And the Egyptians shall know that I am the LORD...
speaker: God
command_summary: God declares He will harden Pharaoh's heart
interpretation_notes: Repeated theological motif throughout Exodus.

full_scripture: When Pharaoh shall speak unto you, saying, Shew a miracle for you: then thou shalt say unto Aaron, Take thy rod, and cast it before Pharaoh...
speaker: God
command_summary: Aaron must cast down the rod before Pharaoh
interpretation_notes: Conditional instruction initiating miraculous authentication.

full_scripture: Get thee unto Pharaoh in the morning... and thou shalt say unto him, The LORD God of the Hebrews hath sent me unto thee, saying, Let my people go...
speaker: God
conditions: At the Nile in the morning
command_summary: Moses must confront Pharaoh at the river
interpretation_notes: First formal plague warning.

full_scripture: And the LORD spake unto Moses, Say unto Aaron, Take thy rod, and stretch out thine hand upon the waters of Egypt...
speaker: God
command_summary: Aaron must stretch his rod over Egypt's waters
interpretation_notes: Miraculous plague enactment command.

full_scripture: And the LORD spake unto Moses, Go unto Pharaoh, and say unto him, Thus saith the LORD, Let my people go, that they may serve me.
speaker: God
command_summary: Tell Pharaoh to let Israel go
interpretation_notes: Repeated divine demand throughout plague narrative.

full_scripture: And if thou refuse to let them go, behold, I will smite all thy borders with frogs:
speaker: God
conditions: If Pharaoh refuses to release Israel
command_summary: Warning of judgment for refusing Israel's release
interpretation_notes: Conditional judgment formula common in plague cycles.

full_scripture: And the LORD spake unto Moses, Say unto Aaron, Stretch forth thine hand with thy rod over the streams, over the rivers, and over the ponds, and cause frogs to come up upon the land of Egypt.
speaker: God
command_summary: Command Aaron to stretch out his rod over Egypt
interpretation_notes: Aaron acts as mediating instrument of divine judgment.

full_scripture: Then Pharaoh called for Moses and Aaron, and said, Intreat the LORD, that he may take away the frogs from me, and from my people; and I will let the people go, that they may do sacrifice unto the LORD.
speaker: Pharaoh
command_summary: Ask Moses and Aaron to pray for removal of frogs
interpretation_notes: Pharaoh temporarily yields under pressure of judgment.

full_scripture: And Moses said unto Pharaoh, Glory over me: when shall I intreat for thee, and for thy servants, and for thy people, to destroy the frogs from thee and thy houses, that they may remain in the river only?
speaker: Moses
command_summary: Ask Pharaoh when prayer should be made
interpretation_notes: Not a command proper but part of intercessory exchange.

full_scripture: And the LORD said unto Moses, Say unto Aaron, Stretch out thy rod, and smite the dust of the land, that it may become lice throughout all the land of Egypt.
speaker: God
command_summary: Command Aaron to strike the dust of Egypt
interpretation_notes: Another mediated plague action through Aaron's rod.

full_scripture: And the LORD said unto Moses, Rise up early in the morning, and stand before Pharaoh; lo, he cometh forth to the water; and say unto him, Thus saith the LORD, Let my people go, that they may serve me.
speaker: God
conditions: When Pharaoh comes to the water
command_summary: Confront Pharaoh again and demand Israel's release
interpretation_notes: Direct repetition of covenant-release demand.

full_scripture: Else, if thou wilt not let my people go, behold, I will send swarms of flies upon thee, and upon thy servants, and upon thy people, and into thy houses.
speaker: God
conditions: If Pharaoh refuses to release Israel
command_summary: Warning of plague of flies
interpretation_notes: Conditional plague escalation pattern.

full_scripture: And Pharaoh called for Moses and for Aaron, and said, Go ye, sacrifice to your God in the land.
speaker: Pharaoh
command_summary: Permit sacrifice within Egypt
interpretation_notes: Compromise proposal from Pharaoh.

full_scripture: And Moses said, It is not meet so to do; for we shall sacrifice the abomination of the Egyptians to the LORD our God.
speaker: Moses
command_summary: Reject sacrifice compromise inside Egypt
interpretation_notes: Moses rejects partial obedience arrangement.

full_scripture: Then the LORD said unto Moses, Go in unto Pharaoh, and tell him, Thus saith the LORD God of the Hebrews, Let my people go, that they may serve me.
speaker: God
command_summary: Let Israel go to serve God
interpretation_notes: Repeated divine demand to Pharaoh during plague narratives.

full_scripture: Behold, the hand of the LORD is upon thy cattle which is in the field.
speaker: God
command_summary: God announces judgment on Egyptian livestock
interpretation_notes: Formal plague warning attached to covenant conflict.

full_scripture: And the LORD said unto Moses and unto Aaron, Take to you handfuls of ashes of the furnace, and let Moses sprinkle it toward the heaven in the sight of Pharaoh.
speaker: God
command_summary: Scatter furnace ashes before Pharaoh
interpretation_notes: Symbolic plague action initiating boils judgment.

full_scripture: Rise up early in the morning, and stand before Pharaoh, and say unto him, Thus saith the LORD God of the Hebrews, Let my people go, that they may serve me.
speaker: God
command_summary: Moses must confront Pharaoh again
interpretation_notes: Continued prophetic confrontation pattern.

full_scripture: Send therefore now, and gather thy cattle, and all that thou hast in the field.
speaker: God
command_summary: Bring livestock under shelter
interpretation_notes: Merciful warning before catastrophic hail judgment.

full_scripture: And the LORD said unto Moses, Stretch forth thine hand toward heaven, that there may be hail in all the land of Egypt.
speaker: God
command_summary: Stretch out hand to bring hail
interpretation_notes: Miraculous prophetic sign act releasing plague.

full_scripture: Entreat the LORD (for it is enough) that there be no more mighty thunderings and hail.
speaker: Pharaoh
command_summary: Ask God to stop the hail
interpretation_notes: Pharaoh requests intercession but without genuine repentance.

full_scripture: As soon as I am gone out of the city, I will spread abroad my hands unto the LORD.
speaker: Moses
command_summary: Moses promises to pray to God
interpretation_notes: Moses mediates before God despite Pharaoh’s hardness.

full_scripture: Go in unto Pharaoh: for I have hardened his heart, and the heart of his servants.
speaker: God
command_summary: Go before Pharaoh
interpretation_notes: Part of the recurring confrontation cycle between Moses and Pharaoh.

full_scripture: That thou mayest tell in the ears of thy son, and of thy son's son, what things I have wrought in Egypt.
speaker: God
command_summary: Teach future generations about God's works
interpretation_notes: Expresses covenantal transmission of God's acts across generations.

full_scripture: Thus saith the LORD God of the Hebrews, How long wilt thou refuse to humble thyself before me? let my people go, that they may serve me.
speaker: Moses
command_summary: Let Israel go to serve God
interpretation_notes: Repeated covenant demand delivered through Moses to Pharaoh.

full_scripture: Else, if thou refuse to let my people go, behold, tomorrow will I bring the locusts into thy coast.
speaker: God
conditions: if Pharaoh refuses to release Israel
command_summary: Locust judgment threatened for disobedience
interpretation_notes: Conditional plague warning tied directly to Pharaoh's refusal.

full_scripture: Let the men go, that they may serve the LORD their God.
speaker: Pharaohs servants
command_summary: Release the men to worship God
interpretation_notes: Egyptian servants recognize the severity of divine judgment.

full_scripture: Go, serve the LORD your God: but who are they that shall go?
speaker: Pharaoh
command_summary: Go serve the LORD under Pharaoh's terms
interpretation_notes: Partial concession by Pharaoh while maintaining control.

full_scripture: We will go with our young and with our old, with our sons and with our daughters.
speaker: Moses
conditions: because Israel must hold a feast unto the LORD
command_summary: All Israel will go worship the LORD
interpretation_notes: Moses insists the entire covenant community must participate.

full_scripture: Not so: go now ye that are men, and serve the LORD.
speaker: Pharaoh
command_summary: Only the men may go worship
interpretation_notes: Pharaoh attempts to limit Israel's worship and movement.

full_scripture: Stretch out thine hand over the land of Egypt for the locusts.
speaker: God
command_summary: Stretch out hand for locust plague
interpretation_notes: Miraculous plague execution command.

full_scripture: Forgive, I pray thee, my sin only this once, and intreat the LORD your God, that he may take away from me this death only.
speaker: Pharaoh
command_summary: Ask God to remove the plague
interpretation_notes: Temporary repentance expressed under judgment pressure.

full_scripture: And he went out from Pharaoh, and intreated the LORD.
speaker: Moses
command_summary: Moses intercedes before the LORD
interpretation_notes: Moses acts as mediator between Pharaoh and God.

full_scripture: Stretch out thine hand toward heaven, that there may be darkness over the land of Egypt.
speaker: God
command_summary: Stretch out hand to bring darkness
interpretation_notes: Command initiating the plague of darkness.

full_scripture: Go ye, serve the LORD; only let your flocks and your herds be stayed.
speaker: Pharaoh
command_summary: Go worship but leave livestock behind
interpretation_notes: Another partial compromise attempt by Pharaoh.

full_scripture: Thou must give us also sacrifices and burnt offerings, that we may sacrifice unto the LORD our God.
speaker: Moses
command_summary: Israel must take sacrifices to worship
interpretation_notes: Moses rejects Pharaoh's restriction because worship requires sacrificial resources.

full_scripture: Get thee from me, take heed to thyself, see my face no more.
speaker: Pharaoh
command_summary: Do not appear before Pharaoh again
interpretation_notes: Pharaoh dismisses Moses in anger after failed negotiations.

full_scripture: Thou hast spoken well, I will see thy face again no more.
speaker: Moses
command_summary: Moses accepts Pharaoh's declaration
interpretation_notes: Marks the end of this confrontation cycle.

full_scripture: Yet will I bring one plague more upon Pharaoh, and upon Egypt; afterwards he will let you go hence.
speaker: God
command_summary: One final plague will cause Pharaoh to release Israel
interpretation_notes: Final plague announcement concluding the plague cycle narrative.

full_scripture: When he shall let you go, he shall surely thrust you out hence altogether.
speaker: God
command_summary: Pharaoh will urgently drive Israel out
interpretation_notes: Prophetic declaration of Egypt’s urgent expulsion of Israel.

full_scripture: Speak now in the ears of the people, and let every man borrow of his neighbour, and every woman of her neighbour, jewels of silver, and jewels of gold.
speaker: God
command_summary: Tell Israel to request silver and gold from Egyptians
interpretation_notes: Instruction fulfills earlier promises that Israel would depart Egypt with substance.

full_scripture: And the LORD gave the people favour in the sight of the Egyptians.
speaker: Moses
command_summary: God gave Israel favor among Egyptians
interpretation_notes: Narrative statement emphasizing divine providence before the exodus.

full_scripture: Thus saith the LORD, About midnight will I go out into the midst of Egypt: And all the firstborn in the land of Egypt shall die.
speaker: Moses
command_summary: God announces death of Egypt's firstborn
interpretation_notes: Formal prophetic declaration of the tenth plague.

full_scripture: And there shall be a great cry throughout all the land of Egypt, such as there was none like it, nor shall be like it any more.
speaker: Moses
conditions: following the death of the firstborn
command_summary: Great mourning will fill Egypt
interpretation_notes: Describes the unprecedented grief resulting from divine judgment.

full_scripture: But against any of the children of Israel shall not a dog move his tongue, against man or beast.
speaker: Moses
command_summary: Israel will be protected during the plague
interpretation_notes: Contrasts divine protection of Israel with judgment upon Egypt.

full_scripture: that ye may know how that the LORD doth put a difference between the Egyptians and Israel.
speaker: Moses
command_summary: God distinguishes Israel from Egypt
interpretation_notes: Highlights covenant distinction between Israel and Egypt.

full_scripture: And all these thy servants shall come down unto me, and bow down themselves unto me, saying, Get thee out.
speaker: Moses
command_summary: Egyptian officials will urge Israel to leave
interpretation_notes: Predicts Egyptian surrender and urgent dismissal of Israel.

full_scripture: And the LORD said unto Moses, Pharaoh shall not hearken unto you; that my wonders may be multiplied in the land of Egypt.
speaker: God
command_summary: Pharaoh's refusal will multiply God's wonders
interpretation_notes: Explains divine purpose behind Pharaoh’s continued resistance.

full_scripture: And Moses and Aaron did all these wonders before Pharaoh: and the LORD hardened Pharaoh's heart, so that he would not let the children of Israel go out of his land.
speaker: Moses
command_summary: Pharaoh remained hardened despite the wonders
interpretation_notes: Summary conclusion of the plague narratives before Passover instructions.

full_scripture: This month shall be unto you the beginning of months: it shall be the first month of the year to you.
speaker: God
command_summary: Establish the first month of Israel's calendar
interpretation_notes: Marks Israel's redemption as the beginning of a covenantal calendar.

full_scripture: In the tenth day of this month they shall take to them every man a lamb.
speaker: God
conditions: on the tenth day of the first month
command_summary: Select a lamb for Passover
interpretation_notes: Foundational Passover preparation command.

full_scripture: And if the household be too little for the lamb, let him and his neighbour next unto his house take it according to the number of the souls.
speaker: God
conditions: if the household is too small for a lamb
command_summary: Share the lamb with neighboring households
interpretation_notes: Passover participation organized communally.

full_scripture: Your lamb shall be without blemish, a male of the first year.
speaker: God
command_summary: Choose an unblemished lamb
interpretation_notes: The lamb's perfection anticipates later sacrificial typology.

full_scripture: And ye shall keep it up until the fourteenth day of the same month: and the whole assembly of the congregation of Israel shall kill it in the evening.
speaker: God
command_summary: Keep and slaughter the Passover lamb
interpretation_notes: Central sacrificial act of the Passover ordinance.

full_scripture: And they shall take of the blood, and strike it on the two side posts and on the upper door post of the houses.
speaker: God
command_summary: Apply the lamb's blood to the doorway
interpretation_notes: The blood sign protects households from judgment.

full_scripture: And they shall eat the flesh in that night, roast with fire, and unleavened bread; and with bitter herbs they shall eat it.
speaker: God
command_summary: Eat the Passover meal with unleavened bread and bitter herbs
interpretation_notes: Meal elements symbolize affliction and haste.

full_scripture: Eat not of it raw, nor sodden at all with water, but roast with fire.
speaker: God
command_summary: Do not eat the lamb raw or boiled
interpretation_notes: Preparation restrictions preserve ritual specificity.

full_scripture: And ye shall let nothing of it remain until the morning; and that which remaineth of it until the morning ye shall burn with fire.
speaker: God
command_summary: Leave none of the Passover lamb until morning
interpretation_notes: Sacred meal must be consumed fully or destroyed.

full_scripture: And thus shall ye eat it; with your loins girded, your shoes on your feet, and your staff in your hand; and ye shall eat it in haste: it is the LORD'S passover.
speaker: God
command_summary: Eat the Passover in readiness and haste
interpretation_notes: Meal posture reflects imminent deliverance.

full_scripture: Sanctify unto me all the firstborn, whatsoever openeth the womb among the children of Israel, both of man and of beast: it is mine.
speaker: God
command_summary: Consecrate all firstborn to God
interpretation_notes: Foundational firstborn consecration command following the death of Egypt's firstborn.

full_scripture: Remember this day, in which ye came out from Egypt, out of the house of bondage.
speaker: Moses
command_summary: Remember the day of deliverance from Egypt
interpretation_notes: Israel is commanded to memorialize divine redemption from slavery.

full_scripture: There shall no leavened bread be eaten.
speaker: Moses
command_summary: Do not eat leavened bread
interpretation_notes: Passover-related prohibition reinforcing separation from Egypt.

full_scripture: Thou shalt keep this service in this month.
speaker: Moses
conditions: when brought into the promised land
command_summary: Observe the Passover service annually
interpretation_notes: Institutional continuation of Passover observance in Canaan.

full_scripture: Seven days thou shalt eat unleavened bread, and in the seventh day shall be a feast to the LORD.
speaker: Moses
command_summary: Eat unleavened bread for seven days
interpretation_notes: Central ritual command for the Feast of Unleavened Bread.

full_scripture: Unleavened bread shall be eaten seven days; and there shall no leavened bread be seen with thee, neither shall there be leaven seen with thee in all thy quarters.
speaker: Moses
command_summary: Remove all leaven during the feast
interpretation_notes: Leaven removal symbolizes separation and purification.

full_scripture: Thou shalt shew thy son in that day, saying, This is done because of that which the LORD did unto me when I came forth out of Egypt.
speaker: Moses
conditions: when children ask about the observance
command_summary: Teach children the meaning of Passover
interpretation_notes: Intergenerational transmission of covenant memory.

full_scripture: It shall be for a sign unto thee upon thine hand, and for a memorial between thine eyes, that the LORD'S law may be in thy mouth.
speaker: Moses
command_summary: Keep redemption as a continual memorial
interpretation_notes: Memorial language later associated with phylactery traditions.

full_scripture: Thou shalt therefore keep this ordinance in his season from year to year.
speaker: Moses
command_summary: Keep the ordinance yearly
interpretation_notes: Formal perpetual observance command.

full_scripture: Thou shalt set apart unto the LORD all that openeth the matrix, and every firstling that cometh of a beast which thou hast; the males shall be the LORD'S.
speaker: Moses
command_summary: Dedicate firstborn males to God
interpretation_notes: Expansion of the earlier sanctification command.

full_scripture: And every firstling of an ass thou shalt redeem with a lamb; and if thou wilt not redeem it, then thou shalt break his neck: and all the firstborn of man among thy children shalt thou redeem.
speaker: Moses
conditions: if redeeming an unclean animal or firstborn son
command_summary: Redeem firstborn sons and unclean animals
interpretation_notes: Introduces redemption substitution principle tied to firstborn theology.

full_scripture: And it shall be when thy son asketh thee in time to come, saying, What is this? that thou shalt say unto him, By strength of hand the LORD brought us out from Egypt, from the house of bondage:
speaker: Moses
conditions: when children ask about the ritual
command_summary: Explain redemption to future generations
interpretation_notes: Covenant remembrance is transmitted through parental teaching.

full_scripture: And it shall be for a token upon thine hand, and for frontlets between thine eyes: for by strength of hand the LORD brought us forth out of Egypt.
speaker: Moses
command_summary: Keep redemption before your eyes continually
interpretation_notes: Symbolic memorial language tied to covenant identity.

full_scripture: Speak unto the children of Israel, that they turn and encamp before Pi-hahiroth, between Migdol and the sea, over against Baal-zephon.
speaker: God
conditions: during Israel's wilderness departure from Egypt
command_summary: Tell Israel to turn and camp by the sea
interpretation_notes: God strategically positions Israel before the Red Sea to provoke Pharaoh's pursuit and demonstrate divine deliverance.

full_scripture: And I will harden Pharaoh's heart, that he shall follow after them; and I will be honoured upon Pharaoh, and upon all his host.
speaker: God
conditions: after Israel camps by the sea
command_summary: God will gain honor through Pharaoh's pursuit
interpretation_notes: God declares His purpose in Pharaoh's pursuit: the revelation of divine glory through judgment.

full_scripture: Fear ye not, stand still, and see the salvation of the LORD.
speaker: Moses
conditions: while pursued by Egypt at the Red Sea
command_summary: Do not fear and witness the LORD's salvation
interpretation_notes: Moses exhorts Israel to trust God instead of fearing the Egyptian army.

full_scripture: The LORD shall fight for you, and ye shall hold your peace.
speaker: Moses
command_summary: Remain silent while the LORD fights for Israel
interpretation_notes: Israel is commanded to cease fearful striving and trust in divine intervention.

full_scripture: Speak unto the children of Israel, that they go forward:
speaker: God
conditions: with the sea before them and Egypt behind them
command_summary: Command Israel to move forward
interpretation_notes: God commands movement in faith before the sea is divided.

full_scripture: Lift thou up thy rod, and stretch out thine hand over the sea, and divide it.
speaker: God
conditions: before the crossing of the Red Sea
command_summary: Stretch out the rod over the sea
interpretation_notes: Moses acts as God's instrument in the miraculous division of the sea.

full_scripture: And the Egyptians shall know that I am the LORD, when I have gotten me honour upon Pharaoh, upon his chariots, and upon his horsemen.
speaker: God
conditions: through the destruction of Egypt's army
command_summary: Egypt will know that the LORD is God
interpretation_notes: The Red Sea judgment functions as revelation to Egypt as well as deliverance for Israel.

full_scripture: Stretch out thine hand over the sea, that the waters may come again upon the Egyptians.
speaker: God
conditions: after Israel safely crosses the sea
command_summary: Stretch out the hand to return the waters
interpretation_notes: The sea is returned upon Egypt in final judicial destruction.

full_scripture: And the people feared the LORD, and believed the LORD, and his servant Moses.
speaker: Moses
conditions: after witnessing the destruction of Egypt
command_summary: Israel feared and believed the LORD
interpretation_notes: Narrative response statement showing covenant trust produced by divine deliverance.

full_scripture: I will sing unto the LORD, for he hath triumphed gloriously: the horse and his rider hath he thrown into the sea.
speaker: Moses
conditions: after deliverance through the Red Sea
command_summary: Sing to the LORD for His triumph
interpretation_notes: Volitional declaration of worship functioning as a model response to divine deliverance.

full_scripture: The LORD is my strength and song, and he is become my salvation: he is my God, and I will prepare him an habitation.
speaker: Moses
command_summary: Prepare a habitation for the LORD
interpretation_notes: Statement of covenant devotion and intention to honor God's dwelling presence.

full_scripture: Who is like unto thee, O LORD, among the gods? who is like thee, glorious in holiness, fearful in praises, doing wonders?
speaker: Moses
command_summary: Declare the uniqueness of the LORD
interpretation_notes: Rhetorical praise emphasizing divine incomparability and holiness.

full_scripture: Thou in thy mercy hast led forth the people which thou hast redeemed: thou hast guided them in thy strength unto thy holy habitation.
speaker: Moses
command_summary: God leads His redeemed people
interpretation_notes: Celebrates covenant redemption and divine guidance.

full_scripture: Thou shalt bring them in, and plant them in the mountain of thine inheritance.
speaker: Moses
command_summary: God will plant Israel in His inheritance
interpretation_notes: Forward-looking covenant promise anticipating settlement in the promised land.

full_scripture: The LORD shall reign for ever and ever.
speaker: Moses
command_summary: The LORD reigns forever
interpretation_notes: Foundational kingship declaration echoed throughout later biblical worship literature.

full_scripture: Sing ye to the LORD, for he hath triumphed gloriously; the horse and his rider hath he thrown into the sea.
speaker: Miriam
conditions: after the destruction of Pharaoh's army
command_summary: Sing to the LORD for His victory
interpretation_notes: Direct worship exhortation echoing the earlier Song of Moses refrain.

full_scripture: If thou wilt diligently hearken to the voice of the LORD thy God, and wilt do that which is right in his sight, and wilt give ear to his commandments, and keep all his statutes, I will put none of these diseases upon thee.
speaker: God
conditions: if Israel obeys God's commandments and statutes
command_summary: Hear and obey the LORD's commandments
interpretation_notes: Foundational covenant obedience formula linking obedience with divine protection.

full_scripture: for I am the LORD that healeth thee.
speaker: God
command_summary: The LORD is Israel's healer
interpretation_notes: Reveals divine covenant identity tied to healing and preservation.

full_scripture: And they took their journey from Elim, and all the congregation of the children of Israel came unto the wilderness of Sin... and the whole congregation of the children of Israel murmured against Moses and Aaron in the wilderness.
speaker: Israelites
command_summary: Israel complains against Moses and Aaron because of hunger
interpretation_notes: This introduces the wilderness testing motif and Israel's repeated murmuring.

full_scripture: Then said the LORD unto Moses, Behold, I will rain bread from heaven for you...
speaker: God
conditions: daily gathering except preparation before sabbath
command_summary: Gather manna daily according to God's instruction
interpretation_notes: The manna test teaches reliance on God's daily provision.

full_scripture: And Moses and Aaron said unto all the children of Israel, At even, then ye shall know that the LORD hath brought you out from the land of Egypt.
speaker: Moses
command_summary: Israel will know the LORD through His provision
interpretation_notes: Provision functions as covenant confirmation.

full_scripture: Come near before the LORD: for he hath heard your murmurings.
speaker: Moses
command_summary: Come before the LORD because He has heard the complaints
interpretation_notes: The assembly is called into God's presence despite rebellion.

full_scripture: This is the bread which the LORD hath given you to eat. This is the thing which the LORD hath commanded...
speaker: Moses
command_summary: Gather manna according to each person's need
interpretation_notes: Manna distribution teaches dependence and equality.

full_scripture: Let no man leave of it till the morning. Notwithstanding they hearkened not unto Moses...
speaker: Moses
command_summary: Do not keep manna overnight
interpretation_notes: Keeping manna overnight violated dependence upon daily provision.

full_scripture: On the sixth day they gathered twice as much bread... Tomorrow is the rest of the holy sabbath unto the LORD.
speaker: Moses
command_summary: Gather double before the Sabbath and rest on the seventh day
interpretation_notes: This is the first explicit Sabbath-rest instruction after creation.

full_scripture: And it came to pass, that there went out some of the people on the seventh day for to gather, and they found none.
speaker: God
command_summary: Do not gather manna on the Sabbath
interpretation_notes: Violation of Sabbath revealed continued distrust.

full_scripture: Fill an omer of it to be kept for your generations...
speaker: Moses
command_summary: Preserve manna as a memorial for future generations
interpretation_notes: The preserved manna functions as covenant testimony.

full_scripture: Wherefore the people did chide with Moses, and said, Give us water that we may drink. And Moses said unto them, Why chide ye with me? wherefore do ye tempt the LORD?
speaker: Moses
conditions: during Israel's wilderness journey without water
command_summary: Do not tempt the LORD through unbelief and contention
interpretation_notes: Moses rebukes Israel for testing the LORD through grumbling and unbelief.

full_scripture: And the LORD said unto Moses, Go on before the people, and take with thee of the elders of Israel; and thy rod, wherewith thou smotest the river, take in thine hand, and go.
speaker: God
conditions: after the people complained for water
command_summary: Go before the people with the elders and rod
interpretation_notes: God directs Moses publicly before Israel to demonstrate divine provision.

full_scripture: Behold, I will stand before thee there upon the rock in Horeb; and thou shalt smite the rock, and there shall come water out of it, that the people may drink.
speaker: God
command_summary: Strike the rock so water may come forth
interpretation_notes: The rock miracle becomes a major typological event later associated with Christ in 1 Corinthians 10.

full_scripture: And Moses said unto Joshua, Choose us out men, and go out, fight with Amalek.
speaker: Moses
command_summary: Choose men and fight Amalek
interpretation_notes: Joshua receives his first military commission under Moses.

full_scripture: to morrow I will stand on the top of the hill with the rod of God in mine hand.
speaker: Moses
command_summary: Moses will stand with the rod of God during battle
interpretation_notes: Moses' raised rod symbolizes dependence upon divine power in battle.

full_scripture: And the LORD said unto Moses, Write this for a memorial in a book, and rehearse it in the ears of Joshua.
speaker: God
command_summary: Record Amalek's judgment as a memorial
interpretation_notes: This is one of the earliest explicit commands to preserve divine acts in written form.

full_scripture: Hearken now unto my voice, I will give thee counsel, and God shall be with thee: Be thou for the people to God-ward, that thou mayest bring the causes unto God:
speaker: Jethro
conditions: while judging the people of Israel
command_summary: Represent the people before God
interpretation_notes: Jethro instructs Moses regarding delegated leadership and mediation before God.

full_scripture: And thou shalt teach them ordinances and laws, and shalt shew them the way wherein they must walk, and the work that they must do.
speaker: Jethro
command_summary: Teach the people God's laws and proper conduct
interpretation_notes: Moses is commanded to instruct Israel in covenantal living and obedience.

full_scripture: Moreover thou shalt provide out of all the people able men, such as fear God, men of truth, hating covetousness; and place such over them...
speaker: Jethro
command_summary: Appoint qualified and godly leaders
interpretation_notes: Leadership qualifications include fear of God, honesty, and rejection of greed.

full_scripture: And let them judge the people at all seasons...
speaker: Jethro
command_summary: Allow appointed judges to judge the people continually
interpretation_notes: Delegated judges are instructed to handle ordinary disputes among the people.

full_scripture: and it shall be, that every great matter they shall bring unto thee, but every small matter they shall judge...
speaker: Jethro
command_summary: Handle small matters locally and bring difficult matters to Moses
interpretation_notes: A hierarchy of judgment is established to reduce Moses' burden.

full_scripture: If thou shalt do this thing, and God command thee so, then thou shalt be able to endure...
speaker: Jethro
command_summary: Follow this counsel if God confirms it
interpretation_notes: Jethro conditions the counsel upon divine approval, making this partly advisory.

full_scripture: Thus shalt thou say to the house of Jacob, and tell the children of Israel;
speaker: God
conditions: Moses ascending unto God at Sinai
command_summary: Tell the house of Jacob God's words
interpretation_notes: God commands Moses to relay covenant words to Israel.

full_scripture: Ye have seen what I did unto the Egyptians, and how I bare you on eagles' wings, and brought you unto myself.
speaker: God
command_summary: Recall God's deliverance from Egypt
interpretation_notes: Declarative covenant reminder grounding later commands.

full_scripture: Now therefore, if ye will obey my voice indeed, and keep my covenant, then ye shall be a peculiar treasure unto me above all people: for all the earth is mine:
speaker: God
conditions: If Israel obeys God's voice and keeps the covenant
command_summary: Obey God's voice and keep His covenant
interpretation_notes: Core Mosaic covenant condition establishing Israel as treasured people.

full_scripture: And ye shall be unto me a kingdom of priests, and an holy nation.
speaker: God
command_summary: Israel shall be a holy priestly nation
interpretation_notes: Identity declaration later echoed in 1 Peter 2.

full_scripture: Go unto the people, and sanctify them to day and to morrow, and let them wash their clothes,
speaker: God
conditions: Preparation before God's descent on Sinai
command_summary: Sanctify the people and have them wash their clothes
interpretation_notes: External purification symbolizes readiness before holy presence.

full_scripture: And be ready against the third day: for the third day the LORD will come down in the sight of all the people upon mount Sinai.
speaker: God
conditions: Before the LORD descends upon Sinai
command_summary: Be ready for the LORD's appearance
interpretation_notes: Readiness command linked to divine manifestation.

full_scripture: And thou shalt set bounds unto the people round about, saying, Take heed to yourselves, that ye go not up into the mount, or touch the border of it:
speaker: God
conditions: During the LORD's presence on Sinai
command_summary: Do not go up or touch the mountain
interpretation_notes: Boundary laws protect sinful people from holy presence.

full_scripture: whosoever toucheth the mount shall be surely put to death:
speaker: God
command_summary: Execute anyone who touches the mountain
interpretation_notes: Capital consequence reinforces holiness boundary.

full_scripture: There shall not an hand touch it, but he shall surely be stoned, or shot through;
speaker: God
conditions: Upon violation of the mountain boundary
command_summary: Do not touch the offender; execute from a distance
interpretation_notes: Even the violator becomes ceremonially dangerous.

full_scripture: And he said unto the people, Be ready against the third day: come not at your wives.
speaker: Moses
conditions: Preparation before meeting God at Sinai
command_summary: Abstain from sexual relations before the third day
interpretation_notes: Temporary ceremonial abstinence before divine encounter.

full_scripture: Go down, charge the people, lest they break through unto the LORD to gaze, and many of them perish.
speaker: God
command_summary: Warn the people not to break through to gaze
interpretation_notes: Reinforces boundary restrictions around Sinai.

full_scripture: And let the priests also, which come near to the LORD, sanctify themselves, lest the LORD break forth upon them.
speaker: God
command_summary: Priests must sanctify themselves
interpretation_notes: Priests are held to stricter holiness requirements.

full_scripture: Away, get thee down, and thou shalt come up, thou, and Aaron with thee: but let not the priests and the people break through to come up unto the LORD, lest he break forth upon them.
speaker: God
command_summary: Do not let priests or people break through to the LORD
interpretation_notes: Final warning before the giving of the law.

full_scripture: And God spake all these words, saying, I am the LORD thy God, which have brought thee out of the land of Egypt, out of the house of bondage.
speaker: God
command_summary: God identifies Himself as Israel's deliverer
interpretation_notes: Covenant preamble establishing divine authority before commands.

full_scripture: Thou shalt have no other gods before me.
speaker: God
command_summary: Worship no other gods
interpretation_notes: Primary command establishing exclusive covenant loyalty.

full_scripture: Thou shalt not make unto thee any graven image... thou shalt not bow down thyself to them, nor serve them.
speaker: God
command_summary: Do not make or worship idols
interpretation_notes: Prohibits both idol creation and idol worship.

full_scripture: Thou shalt not take the name of the LORD thy God in vain.
speaker: God
command_summary: Do not misuse God's name
interpretation_notes: Includes false oaths and irreverent use of the divine name.

full_scripture: Remember the sabbath day, to keep it holy.
speaker: God
command_summary: Keep the Sabbath holy
interpretation_notes: Sabbath observance rooted in creation order.

full_scripture: Honour thy father and thy mother.
speaker: God
command_summary: Honor parents
interpretation_notes: First commandment with attached promise.

full_scripture: Thou shalt not kill.
speaker: God
command_summary: Do not murder
interpretation_notes: Primarily forbids unlawful killing.

full_scripture: Thou shalt not commit adultery.
speaker: God
command_summary: Do not commit adultery
interpretation_notes: Protects covenantal marriage fidelity.

full_scripture: Thou shalt not steal.
speaker: God
command_summary: Do not steal
interpretation_notes: Protects property and covenant trust.

full_scripture: Thou shalt not bear false witness against thy neighbour.
speaker: God
command_summary: Do not give false testimony
interpretation_notes: Originally judicial but broadly applies to truthful speech.

full_scripture: Thou shalt not covet thy neighbour's house... nor any thing that is thy neighbour's.
speaker: God
command_summary: Do not covet
interpretation_notes: Addresses inward desire rather than external action alone.

full_scripture: Now these are the judgments which thou shalt set before them.
speaker: God
command_summary: Set the judgments before Israel
interpretation_notes: Introductory legal framing statement establishing covenant case law.

full_scripture: If thou buy an Hebrew servant, six years he shall serve: and in the seventh he shall go out free for nothing.
speaker: God
conditions: If an Israelite buys a Hebrew servant
command_summary: Release Hebrew servants in the seventh year
interpretation_notes: Hebrew debt-servitude regulated with mandatory release cycle.

full_scripture: If he came in by himself, he shall go out by himself: if he were married, then his wife shall go out with him.
speaker: God
conditions: Upon release of a Hebrew servant
command_summary: Release servant according to original marital status
interpretation_notes: Maintains legal consistency regarding family status.

full_scripture: And if the servant shall plainly say, I love my master, my wife, and my children; I will not go out free: Then his master shall bring him unto the judges...
speaker: God
conditions: If servant voluntarily remains with master
command_summary: Formally establish permanent servitude by public procedure
interpretation_notes: Voluntary lifelong servitude established before judges as covenantal legal act.

full_scripture: And if a man sell his daughter to be a maidservant, she shall not go out as the menservants do...
speaker: God
conditions: If a daughter is sold as a maidservant
command_summary: Do not mistreat or defraud maidservants
interpretation_notes: Protective legislation regarding female servants and marital obligations.

full_scripture: He that smiteth a man, so that he die, shall be surely put to death.
speaker: God
command_summary: Execute murderers
interpretation_notes: Capital punishment principle for murder under Mosaic law.

full_scripture: And if a man lie not in wait, but God deliver him into his hand; then I will appoint thee a place whither he shall flee.
speaker: God
command_summary: Provide refuge for accidental manslayers
interpretation_notes: Foundation for cities of refuge legislation.

full_scripture: But if a man come presumptuously upon his neighbour, to slay him with guile; thou shalt take him from mine altar, that he may die.
speaker: God
command_summary: Do not spare deliberate murderers
interpretation_notes: Sacred space does not exempt intentional murderers from judgment.

full_scripture: And he that smiteth his father, or his mother, shall be surely put to death.
speaker: God
command_summary: Punish violent assault against parents by death
interpretation_notes: Family authority protected under covenant law.

full_scripture: If a man shall steal an ox, or a sheep, and kill it, or sell it; he shall restore five oxen for an ox, and four sheep for a sheep.
speaker: God
command_summary: Require restitution for stolen livestock
interpretation_notes: Establishes proportional restitution penalties for theft.

full_scripture: If a thief be found breaking up, and be smitten that he die, there shall no blood be shed for him.
speaker: God
conditions: if a thief is killed during a nighttime break-in
command_summary: No bloodguilt for killing a burglar during intrusion
interpretation_notes: Addresses self-defense and legal liability.

full_scripture: If the sun be risen upon him, there shall be blood shed for him; for he should make full restitution.
speaker: God
conditions: if a thief is killed in daylight
command_summary: Require restitution instead of lethal retaliation
interpretation_notes: Differentiates defensive necessity from excessive retaliation.

full_scripture: If a man shall cause a field or vineyard to be eaten... he shall make restitution of the best of his own field.
speaker: God
conditions: if property damage occurs through negligence
command_summary: Make restitution for damaged property
interpretation_notes: Negligent economic harm requires compensation.

full_scripture: Thou shalt not suffer a witch to live.
speaker: God
command_summary: Do not permit sorcery to continue
interpretation_notes: Capital prohibition against occult practices in Israel.

full_scripture: Whosoever lieth with a beast shall surely be put to death.
speaker: God
command_summary: Forbid bestiality under penalty of death
interpretation_notes: Sexual purity law tied to covenant holiness.

full_scripture: He that sacrificeth unto any god, save unto the LORD only, he shall be utterly destroyed.
speaker: God
command_summary: Forbid sacrifice to false gods
interpretation_notes: Exclusive worship of Yahweh required under Mosaic covenant.

full_scripture: Thou shalt neither vex a stranger, nor oppress him: for ye were strangers in the land of Egypt.
speaker: God
command_summary: Do not oppress foreigners
interpretation_notes: Grounds compassion in Israel's own historical suffering.

full_scripture: Ye shall not afflict any widow, or fatherless child.
speaker: God
command_summary: Do not afflict widows or orphans
interpretation_notes: Protection of vulnerable people is central to covenant ethics.

full_scripture: If thou lend money to any of my people that is poor by thee, thou shalt not be to him as an usurer.
speaker: God
command_summary: Do not charge oppressive interest to the poor
interpretation_notes: Protects the economically vulnerable from exploitation.

full_scripture: Thou shalt not raise a false report: put not thine hand with the wicked to be an unrighteous witness.
speaker: God
command_summary: Do not spread false reports or join wicked testimony
interpretation_notes: Addresses both slander and participation in corrupt legal testimony.

full_scripture: Thou shalt not follow a multitude to do evil; neither shalt thou speak in a cause to decline after many to wrest judgment.
speaker: God
command_summary: Do not follow the crowd into evil or corrupt judgment
interpretation_notes: Focuses on resisting mob influence and preserving impartial justice.

full_scripture: Neither shalt thou countenance a poor man in his cause.
speaker: God
command_summary: Do not show favoritism to the poor in judgment
interpretation_notes: Justice must remain impartial even toward the disadvantaged.

full_scripture: If thou meet thine enemy's ox or his ass going astray, thou shalt surely bring it back to him again.
speaker: God
conditions: if an enemy's animal is found wandering
command_summary: Return a wandering animal to your enemy
interpretation_notes: Shows active mercy toward enemies through practical assistance.

full_scripture: If thou see the ass of him that hateth thee lying under his burden, and wouldest forbear to help him, thou shalt surely help with him.
speaker: God
conditions: if an enemy's animal collapses under burden
command_summary: Help your enemy with his burdened animal
interpretation_notes: Requires overcoming personal hostility to provide aid.

full_scripture: Thou shalt not wrest the judgment of thy poor in his cause.
speaker: God
command_summary: Do not deny justice to the poor
interpretation_notes: Balances verse 3 by prohibiting oppression of the poor.

full_scripture: Keep thee far from a false matter; and the innocent and righteous slay thou not: for I will not justify the wicked.
speaker: God
command_summary: Avoid falsehood and do not condemn the innocent
interpretation_notes: Combines moral separation from deceit with judicial protection of innocents.

full_scripture: And thou shalt take no gift: for the gift blindeth the wise, and perverteth the words of the righteous.
speaker: God
command_summary: Do not accept bribes
interpretation_notes: Bribery is condemned because it corrupts discernment and justice.

full_scripture: Also thou shalt not oppress a stranger: for ye know the heart of a stranger, seeing ye were strangers in the land of Egypt.
speaker: God
command_summary: Do not oppress foreigners
interpretation_notes: Israel's Egyptian history is used as motivation for compassion toward strangers.

full_scripture: And six years thou shalt sow thy land, and shalt gather in the fruits thereof: But the seventh year thou shalt let it rest and lie still; that the poor of thy people may eat.
speaker: God
command_summary: Observe the sabbath year for the land
interpretation_notes: Land rest laws combine stewardship, mercy, and covenantal sabbath principles.

full_scripture: Six days thou shalt do thy work, and on the seventh day thou shalt rest.
speaker: God
command_summary: Rest on the seventh day
interpretation_notes: Weekly sabbath rest extends to servants and animals in the broader verse.

full_scripture: And in all things that I have said unto you be circumspect: and make no mention of the name of other gods.
speaker: God
command_summary: Be careful to obey and avoid honoring other gods
interpretation_notes: Forbids verbal acknowledgment associated with pagan worship.

full_scripture: Three times thou shalt keep a feast unto me in the year.
speaker: God
command_summary: Observe the yearly feasts before the LORD
interpretation_notes: Refers collectively to Passover, Pentecost, and Tabernacles.

full_scripture: Thou shalt not offer the blood of my sacrifice with leavened bread.
speaker: God
command_summary: Do not offer sacrifice with leaven
interpretation_notes: Connected to Passover purity regulations.

full_scripture: The first of the firstfruits of thy land thou shalt bring into the house of the LORD thy God.
speaker: God
command_summary: Bring firstfruits to the LORD
interpretation_notes: Represents dedicating the first and best produce to God.

full_scripture: Thou shalt not seethe a kid in his mother's milk.
speaker: God
command_summary: Do not boil a young goat in its mother's milk
interpretation_notes: Meaning debated; likely connected to pagan ritual separation or symbolic holiness.

full_scripture: Beware of him, and obey his voice, provoke him not.
speaker: God
conditions: while following the angel sent before Israel
command_summary: Obey the angel sent before you
interpretation_notes: The angel functions as divine representative with covenant authority.

full_scripture: Thou shalt not bow down to their gods, nor serve them, nor do after their works.
speaker: God
command_summary: Do not worship or imitate pagan gods
interpretation_notes: Prohibits both worship and imitation of pagan religious systems.

full_scripture: And ye shall serve the LORD your God.
speaker: God
command_summary: Serve the LORD your God
interpretation_notes: Central covenant loyalty statement contrasting pagan worship.

full_scripture: Thou shalt make no covenant with them, nor with their gods.
speaker: God
command_summary: Make no covenant with pagan nations or gods
interpretation_notes: Forbids political and religious alliances that compromise covenant fidelity.

full_scripture: They shall not dwell in thy land, lest they make thee sin against me.
speaker: God
command_summary: Do not allow idolatrous influence to remain
interpretation_notes: Functions as covenant warning tied to spiritual corruption.

full_scripture: And he said unto Moses, Come up unto the LORD, thou, and Aaron, Nadab, and Abihu, and seventy of the elders of Israel; and worship ye afar off. And Moses alone shall come near the LORD: but they shall not come nigh; neither shall the people go up with him.
speaker: God
conditions: while approaching Mount Sinai before covenant ratification
command_summary: Come before the LORD and worship from afar
interpretation_notes: The command establishes hierarchical access to God's presence under the Mosaic covenant.

full_scripture: And Moses came and told the people all the words of the LORD, and all the judgments: and all the people answered with one voice, and said, All the words which the LORD hath said will we do.
speaker: Moses
command_summary: Commit to obey all words of the LORD
interpretation_notes: Israel corporately affirms covenant obedience.

full_scripture: And Moses wrote all the words of the LORD, and rose up early in the morning, and builded an altar under the hill, and twelve pillars, according to the twelve tribes of Israel.
speaker: Moses
command_summary: Record the words of the LORD and establish covenant memorials
interpretation_notes: Moses models covenant preservation through writing and symbolic representation.

full_scripture: And he sent young men of the children of Israel, which offered burnt offerings, and sacrificed peace offerings of oxen unto the LORD.
speaker: Moses
command_summary: Offer sacrifices unto the LORD
interpretation_notes: The sacrifices function as covenant confirmation offerings.

full_scripture: And he took the book of the covenant, and read in the audience of the people: and they said, All that the LORD hath said will we do, and be obedient.
speaker: Moses
conditions: after hearing the book of the covenant
command_summary: Promise obedience to the covenant
interpretation_notes: This restates Israel's covenant commitment after formal reading.

full_scripture: And Moses took the blood, and sprinkled it on the people, and said, Behold the blood of the covenant, which the LORD hath made with you concerning all these words.
speaker: Moses
command_summary: Confirm the covenant through blood
interpretation_notes: This passage becomes foundational for later covenant-blood theology in Hebrews.

full_scripture: And the LORD said unto Moses, Come up to me into the mount, and be there: and I will give thee tables of stone, and a law, and commandments which I have written; that thou mayest teach them.
speaker: God
command_summary: Come up the mountain to receive and teach God's law
interpretation_notes: Moses is commissioned as mediator and teacher of divine law.

full_scripture: And Moses rose up, and his minister Joshua: and Moses went up into the mount of God. And he said unto the elders, Tarry ye here for us, until we come again unto you.
speaker: Moses
command_summary: Remain and wait until Moses returns
interpretation_notes: Moses delegates temporary authority to the elders during his absence.

full_scripture: And, behold, Aaron and Hur are with you: if any man have any matters to do, let him come unto them.
speaker: Moses
command_summary: Bring disputes to Aaron and Hur
interpretation_notes: An early delegated judicial structure within Israel.

full_scripture: Speak unto the children of Israel, that they bring me an offering: of every man that giveth it willingly with his heart ye shall take my offering.
speaker: God
command_summary: Receive willing offerings from Israel
interpretation_notes: Establishes voluntary giving principle for tabernacle construction.

full_scripture: And let them make me a sanctuary; that I may dwell among them.
speaker: God
command_summary: Build a sanctuary for God
interpretation_notes: Central command initiating the tabernacle system and divine dwelling motif.

full_scripture: According to all that I shew thee, after the pattern of the tabernacle, and the pattern of all the instruments thereof, even so shall ye make it.
speaker: God
command_summary: Construct the tabernacle according to God's pattern
interpretation_notes: Emphasizes obedience to divine heavenly pattern.

full_scripture: And they shall make an ark of shittim wood... And thou shalt put into the ark the testimony which I shall give thee.
speaker: God
command_summary: Construct the ark and place the testimony inside
interpretation_notes: Includes ark construction and placement of covenant testimony.

full_scripture: And thou shalt make a mercy seat of pure gold... And there I will meet with thee, and I will commune with thee from above the mercy seat.
speaker: God
conditions: with cherubims overshadowing the mercy seat
command_summary: Make the mercy seat and cherubim
interpretation_notes: Mercy seat becomes central atonement and divine meeting location.

full_scripture: Thou shalt also make a table of shittim wood... And thou shalt set upon the table shewbread before me alway.
speaker: God
command_summary: Construct the table and set the shewbread continually
interpretation_notes: The continual bread symbolizes covenant fellowship and provision.

full_scripture: And thou shalt make a candlestick of pure gold... And look that thou make them after their pattern, which was shewed thee in the mount.
speaker: God
command_summary: Make the golden candlestick according to the pattern
interpretation_notes: Lampstand imagery later echoes temple and Revelation symbolism.

full_scripture: Moreover thou shalt make the tabernacle with ten curtains of fine twined linen, and blue, and purple, and scarlet: with cherubims of cunning work shalt thou make them.
speaker: God
command_summary: Make the tabernacle curtains with fine linen and cherubim
interpretation_notes: Instruction establishes the sacred structure and symbolic holiness of the tabernacle.

full_scripture: The five curtains shall be coupled together one to another; and other five curtains shall be coupled one to another.
speaker: God
command_summary: Couple the curtains together into one tabernacle covering
interpretation_notes: The unified curtains symbolize the unity and completeness of God's dwelling.

full_scripture: And thou shalt make curtains of goats' hair to be a covering upon the tabernacle.
speaker: God
command_summary: Make protective coverings for the tabernacle
interpretation_notes: Outer coverings provided protection while maintaining sacred separation.

full_scripture: And thou shalt make boards for the tabernacle of shittim wood standing up.
speaker: God
conditions: during construction of the tabernacle frame
command_summary: Construct the tabernacle boards and framework
interpretation_notes: Structural integrity reflects ordered worship and divine design.

full_scripture: And thou shalt make bars of shittim wood; five for the boards of the one side of the tabernacle.
speaker: God
conditions: during assembly of the tabernacle structure
command_summary: Make bars to bind the tabernacle structure together
interpretation_notes: The bars unify the structure and preserve stability in worship.

full_scripture: And thou shalt make a vail of blue, and purple, and scarlet, and fine twined linen of cunning work.
speaker: God
conditions: to divide the holy place from the most holy
command_summary: Make the veil separating the holy places
interpretation_notes: The veil symbolizes separation between sinful humanity and God's holiness.

full_scripture: And thou shalt make an hanging for the door of the tent.
speaker: God
conditions: during completion of the tabernacle entrance
command_summary: Make the hanging for the entrance of the tabernacle
interpretation_notes: The entrance hanging reinforces controlled access to sacred space.

full_scripture: And thou shalt make an altar of shittim wood, five cubits long, and five cubits broad; the altar shall be foursquare: and the height thereof shall be three cubits...
speaker: God
conditions: for construction of the tabernacle altar
command_summary: Construct the bronze altar for burnt offerings
interpretation_notes: Detailed altar specifications connected to sacrificial worship under the Mosaic covenant.

full_scripture: And thou shalt make the court of the tabernacle: for the south side southward there shall be hangings for the court of fine twined linen...
speaker: God
command_summary: Construct the outer court of the tabernacle
interpretation_notes: The court establishes separation and sacred boundaries around worship space.

full_scripture: And thou shalt command the children of Israel, that they bring thee pure oil olive beaten for the light, to cause the lamp to burn always.
speaker: God
conditions: for maintaining continual lamp light in the tabernacle
command_summary: Bring pure olive oil for the continual lamp
interpretation_notes: The continual lamp symbolizes perpetual worship and covenant presence before God.

full_scripture: God commands Moses to bring Aaron and his sons near to serve as priests, and to make holy garments for glory and beauty.
speaker: God
conditions: when consecrating priesthood under Mosaic covenant
command_summary: Set apart Aaron and sons as priests and make holy garments

full_scripture: Instructions for the ephod: materials, design, and shoulder stones bearing the names of Israel's tribes.
speaker: God
command_summary: Make ephod with tribal stones for remembrance before God

full_scripture: Instructions for the breastpiece of judgment with twelve stones representing Israel and the Urim and Thummim.
speaker: God
command_summary: Make breastpiece with judgment stones and Urim/Thummim

full_scripture: Instructions for the robe of the ephod with bells and pomegranates so Aaron will not die when ministering.
speaker: God
command_summary: Make robe with bells for priestly safety in ministry

full_scripture: Instructions for a gold plate engraved 'Holy to the LORD' on Aaron's forehead for bearing Israel's guilt.
speaker: God
command_summary: Make holy crown plate to bear iniquity of offerings

full_scripture: Instructions for tunics, sashes, and garments for Aaron's sons, establishing perpetual priesthood ordination.
speaker: God
command_summary: Clothe Aaron and sons for priesthood service continually

full_scripture: Instructions for consecrating Aaron and his sons as priests: take a young bull and two rams without blemish, unleavened bread, cakes, and wafers; bring them to the tent of meeting. Wash Aaron and his sons, clothe Aaron with priestly garments, and anoint him. Dress his sons and anoint them also. This establishes their priesthood as a perpetual ordinance.
speaker: God
command_summary: Instructions for priestly consecration setup
interpretation_notes: Foundational ordination instructions establishing Levitical priesthood.

full_scripture: Bring the bullock before the tabernacle. Aaron and his sons lay hands on it. The bull is slaughtered as a sin offering. Its blood is applied to the altar horns, poured at the base, and the fat is burned. The flesh is burned outside the camp as a sin offering.
speaker: God
command_summary: Sin offering bull sacrifice procedure
interpretation_notes: Sin removal precedes priestly service.

full_scripture: One ram is taken as a burnt offering. Aaron and sons lay hands on it. The ram is killed and its blood sprinkled around the altar. The animal is cut and entirely burned on the altar as a pleasing aroma to God.
speaker: God
command_summary: Burnt offering ram ritual
interpretation_notes: Whole offering signifies total dedication.

full_scripture: Second ram (ram of ordination) is brought. Blood is placed on Aaron's right ear, thumb, and big toe, and on his sons likewise. Blood is sprinkled on the altar. Portions of the ram are waved before God and burned. This consecrates priests for service.
speaker: God
command_summary: Priestly ordination blood ritual
interpretation_notes: Blood on ear/hand/foot signifies sanctified hearing, action, and walk.

full_scripture: Priestly garments are passed from Aaron to his sons after his death. The priesthood continues through generations.
speaker: God
command_summary: Priestly succession established
interpretation_notes: Establishes hereditary priesthood.

full_scripture: Instructions for eating the ram of ordination at the holy place. Anything left is burned. Consecration lasts seven days. The altar is purified and becomes most holy.
speaker: God
command_summary: Seven-day consecration completion rules
interpretation_notes: Seven-day sanctification pattern establishes sacred time.

full_scripture: Daily offerings of two lambs: morning and evening. Grain and drink offerings accompany them. God promises to dwell among Israel and be their God, sanctifying the tabernacle.
speaker: God
command_summary: Daily continual offerings and divine presence promise
interpretation_notes: Climactic covenant promise: God's dwelling among His people.

full_scripture: Altar of incense instruction (KJV Exodus 30:1-10)
speaker: God
command_summary: Make altar of incense and burn incense continually

full_scripture: Atonement money instruction (KJV Exodus 30:11-16)
speaker: God
command_summary: Each person gives ransom money for atonement

full_scripture: Bronze basin washing instruction (KJV Exodus 30:17-21)
speaker: God
command_summary: Priests must wash hands and feet before service

full_scripture: Anointing oil formula instruction (KJV Exodus 30:22-33)
speaker: God
command_summary: Make holy anointing oil and consecrate tabernacle

full_scripture: Incense formula instruction (KJV Exodus 30:34-38)
speaker: God
command_summary: Make sacred incense and do not copy it for personal use

full_scripture: And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying, See, I have called by name Bezaleel the son of Uri... And I have filled him with the spirit of God, in wisdom, and in understanding, and in knowledge, and in all manner of workmanship...
speaker: God
command_summary: God appoints and empowers Bezalel for sacred craftsmanship
interpretation_notes: Divine empowerment for craftsmanship shows sacred nature of skilled labor.

full_scripture: And I, behold, I have given with him Aholiab... and in the hearts of all that are wise hearted I have put wisdom...
speaker: God
command_summary: God appoints additional skilled workers and imparts wisdom
interpretation_notes: Wisdom is portrayed as divinely distributed for communal sacred work.

full_scripture: The tabernacle, and the ark of the testimony... and all that I have commanded thee shall they do...
speaker: God
command_summary: Detailed instructions for constructing sacred objects
interpretation_notes: Establishes detailed sacred architecture governed by divine command.

full_scripture: Verily my sabbaths ye shall keep... for it is a sign between me and you throughout your generations...
speaker: God
command_summary: Keep the Sabbath as a covenant sign
interpretation_notes: Sabbath functions as covenant identity marker, not merely rest regulation.

full_scripture: And he gave unto Moses... two tables of testimony, tables of stone, written with the finger of God.
speaker: God
command_summary: God delivers the tablets of the covenant law
interpretation_notes: Divine authorship of law emphasized through 'finger of God' imagery.

full_scripture: Aaron tells the people to give their gold; he fashions a golden calf. The people declare it as the god that brought them out of Egypt and engage in worship and revelry.
speaker: Aaron
command_summary: Make and worship a golden calf

full_scripture: The LORD tells Moses that the people have corrupted themselves; Moses intercedes for Israel, appealing to God's covenant promises.
speaker: God
